The Hollywood Walk of Fame is basically the biggest tourist scam in the U.S tbh.

It’s just a couple sidewalks full of street performers, random hustlers, ppl yelling at nothing, drunk dudes, and homeless ppl just hanging around all day. It looks way more “sad reality” than “Hollywood glam”.

Ngl there’s nothing special abt it in real life—it’s dirty, kinda run-down, and honestly pretty underwhelming. You’d think the city would at least try keep it clean, but it feels like they only do the bare minimum maintenance, if even that.

And unless you’re actually a top-tier celeb, don’t even expect ur star to be treated with respect. There’s a real chance your name ends up on a cracked sidewalk tile with gum stuck on it, trash around, or even like… dog pee on it. Kinda wild.

Let’s face it, it’s a must thing to do in Hollywood. There are thousands of stars to see and they do get a little boring after a while, but if you walk the length of the boulevard you’ll still get to see fresh names you recognise.
The area itself is pretty ok. Not really sure how you can give it a negative review.
No reason to skip it - why would you after flying half the way round the world…
